one_time_description: 1919 Soldier Settlement Board Surrender IR 102 Specific Claim settlement of $127,874,961 received from Canada, with $127,216,180 contributed to a Trust; this is a non-recurring specific claims settlement
economic_notes: Three government business enterprises: Aspen Developments Inc., Muskeg Lake Economic Development Limited Partnership, and MLCN Community Store Ltd. Combined GBE revenue was $28,403,202 with net income of $2,323,719. Nation received equity income of $1,970,104. MLCN Economic Development LP made mortgage payments, distributions, and education payments on behalf of the Nation totalling $583,771.
notes: Fiscal year 2022 (ended March 31, 2022). Dominant item is the $127,216,180 1919 Soldier Settlement Board Surrender specific claim trust contribution, which inflates revenue and accumulated surplus dramatically. This trust was fully consolidated into the financial statements. Three GBEs accounted for on modified equity basis (Aspen Developments Inc., Muskeg Lake Economic Development LP, MLCN Community Store Ltd.) with combined assets of ~$46.9M and long-term debt of ~$29.9M not on the Nation's balance sheet. The prior year (2021) included a $3,331,185 Rebellion Band Claim settlement also treated as one-time. No remuneration schedule was included in the extracted text. Province identified as Saskatchewan based on Saskatoon Tribal Council affiliation and context. ISC clawed back amount was $0 in 2022 (negative $12,612 in 2021). Total government transfers calculated as ISC $9,735,134 + Health Canada $858,781 + CMHC $500,858 = $13,094,773; OSR includes equity income, settlement trust, and other revenue.
